text: The laparoscopic view reveals patent fallopian tubes and visible endometriosis in the pouch of Douglas. The surgeon excises the endometriotic tissue from this area.
The surgeon injects vasopressin into the uterus, followed by making an oblique incision on the uterine surface. The fibroid is then carefully enucleated from the surrounding uterine tissue.
The surgeon sutures the inner layer of the uterine defect in two layers using StrataFix barb suture, followed by suturing the serosa with interrupted polyglycine I sutures.

The surgeon mosellates the fibroid within a containment bag and removes it from the surgical field. Anti-adhesion gel is applied to the excised endometriotic site and the uterine incision to prevent postoperative adhesions.
The photograph taken during the caesarean section shows the healed myomectomy incision on the uterus, with no adhesions visible between the uterus and the abdominal wall or omentum.
